sections.title: Outsourcing risk management framework for financial institutions
sections.summary: Financial institutions must establish comprehensive outsourcing policies before engaging third-party service providers. These policies must define core activities that cannot be outsourced, assess materiality of outsourced activities, evaluate risks, and include contingency plans. The Board of Directors and senior management share responsibilities for oversight, including approving material outsourcing, monitoring risks, and ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements. The framework applies to all financial institutions licensed by the Bank of Mauritius.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates a risk management framework for outsourcing that financial institutions must implement before engaging third-party service providers. It requires specific documentation and approval processes for material outsourcing activities, directly impacting cross-border data transfers through offshoring arrangements.

requirements.text: Financial institutions must establish a comprehensive policy on outsourcing before engaging third-party service providers
The policy must define core activities that cannot be outsourced
The policy must assess materiality of outsourced activities
The policy must evaluate risks associated with outsourced activities
The policy must include contingency plans for business disruptions
The Board of Directors must approve the outsourcing policy
The Board of Directors must assess outsourcing strategies against strategic objectives
The Board of Directors must approve material outsourcing arrangements
The Board of Directors must review material outsourcing activities annually
Senior management must monitor and control outsourcing arrangements
Senior management must ensure contingency plans for service provider changes
Senior management must ensure regulatory compliance in outsourced services

sections.source_quote: Financial institutions should establish a comprehensive policy on outsourcing. The policy should guide the assessment of whether and how an activity should be outsourced. The policy should be well documented and should include, inter alia: strategic goals, objectives and business needs of a financial institution in relation to outsourcing; a clear definition of the range of activities that may be outsourced and those core activities which cannot be outsourced; steps to evaluate whether a particular activity is appropriate for outsourcing; criteria for determining material outsourcing; processes for evaluating risks associated with an outsourced activity; criteria for evaluating outsourcing relationships (with service providers) including necessary controls and reporting processes on an ongoing basis; limits on the acceptable overall level of outsourced activities; eligibility criteria for selecting service providers taking into account any relation, directly or indirectly, with the latter; issues addressing risk concentrations and risks arising from outsourcing multiple activities to the same service provider; steps to ensure compliance with legal and regulatory requirements in both home and host countries; and contingency plan in case of business disruptions.
sections.relevance_reason: Core outsourcing risk management for financial data

sections.heading: (continued)
sections.title: Core functions that cannot be outsourced
sections.summary: [extraction failed: model output unparseable] Financial institutions must not outsource core functions including strategic oversight, internal audit, and compliance. The Bank explicitly prohibits outsourcing these activities but allows case-by-case exceptions for intra-group outsourcing. Such exceptions require prior authorization and must be treated as material outsourcing with the same requirements as in section 4.1.3.
sections.dtia_summary: This section defines core functions that cannot be outsourced, requiring financial institutions to seek Bank authorization for any intra-group outsourcing of these functions, which impacts DTIA by mandating specific safeguards for critical control functions.

requirements.text: Financial institutions must not outsource board and senior management functions such as strategic oversight
Financial institutions must not outsource internal audit function
Financial institutions must not outsource compliance function
Financial institutions seeking to outsource core functions within the group must seek prior authorization from the Bank
Financial institutions must treat intra-group outsourcing of core functions as material outsourcing

sections.source_quote: Activities that are considered ‘core' and should not be outsourced are; board and senior management functions such as strategic oversight; internal audit function; and compliance function.
